DISCOVERY, 302, UNOPPOSED MOTION OF DEFENDANTS AMN HEALTHCARE, INC., NURSEFINDERS, LLC AND TINA REYNOLDS-CURICO TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF JOSEPH WILLIAMS IS GRANTED. MR. WILLIAMS SHALL APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 31, 2015 PURSUANT TO THE AMENDED NOTICE OF TAKING DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF JOSEPH WILLIAMS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, EXHIBIT F TO THE DECLARATION OF AMY WINTERSHEIMER FINDLEY IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION. ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION NOTICE ARE WAIVED. THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS EXTENDED TO AND INCLUDING DECEMBER 31, 2015 ONLY FOR THE PURPOSES OF THIS DEPOSITION. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED FOR FAILURE TO GIVE NOTICE OF FROM WHOM THEY ARE SOUGHT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DENNIS HYDE, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. EVAN M. REESE, ESQ. of Allen Matkins, Tel No. 415-273-8408 appearing for Defendants AMN Healthcare, Inc., Nursefinders, LLC and Tina Reynolds-Curcio;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The unopposed motion of Defendants AMN HEALTHCARE, INC., NURSEFINDERS, LLC and TINA REYNOLDS-CURICO to compel the deposition of Plaintiff JOSEPH WILLIAMS is granted. Mr. WILLIAMS shall appear for his deposition and produce documents on or before December 31, 2015 pursuant to the Amended Notice of Taking Deposition of Plaintiff Joseph Williams and Request for Production of Documents, Exhibit F to the Declaration of Amy Wintersheimer Findley in Support of the motion. Any objections to the deposition notice are waived. The discovery cutoff is extended to and including December 31, 2015 only for the purposes of this deposition. Monetary sanctions are denied for failure to give notice of from whom they are sought. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Dennis Hyde, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BERNE REUBEN, Tel No. (510) 981-0143 appearing for Cross-Complainant Tina Reynolds-Curcio;
Atty. MARK WILSON of Emmett Law, Tel No. (415) 500-1678 appearing for Cross-Defendant Joseph Williams;
Atty. AMY WINTERSHEIMER FINDLEY of Allen Matkins Leck Gamble Mallory & Natsis, LLP, Tel No. (619) 233-1155 appearing telephonically for Cross-Defendants AMN Healthcare, Inc., and Nursefinders, LLC.

RULING - Matter not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH WILLLIAMS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF TINA REYNOLDS-CURCIO is overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) sets forth the alleged harassment in sufficient detail. (FACC, par. 9.) The FACC adequately pleads the frequency, intensity, and/or timeliness of the alleged acts. (FACC, par. 9.) The FACC sufficiently pleads that Cross-Complainant exhausted her administrative remedies. (FACC, par. 8.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
